I always loved this game but they are really dragging it down with those ♥♥♥♥♥♥ microtransactions. Why in lords name whould they ever add an DLC and give it it own button next to play and co-op. Greatest tower defense game ever made ruined by money hungry devs, there was once a time when you could unlock every cosmetic in the game by just being good at events or putting the time in. It's sad how the devs didn't see how important that was to the players, adding hero skins and powers locked behind a pay wall is the dumbest move this company could have made.
the over monetization is scummy, charging 16 dollars for a new hero when they've all been free for the game's entire lifespan feels like a slap in the face

I seriously love Bloons TD 6, It's one of my favorite games in the world ever since it first released. I've always loved monkeys since I was little and when I saw this game on miniclip games I was absolutely astonished. I'd be playing on the monkey bars so that I could roleplay the dart monkey. When I saw bubbles on the michael jackson biopic, I immediately pulled out my phone and started playing BTD 6 while watching the movie on my iphone 12 pro max with 256 gigadarts LMAOOOOO that is the color black, which is such a good color because of EMO GIRLS ROCK.  I had a crush on the girl from danny phantom. If the developers ever add her as a monkey im immediately buying it for my collection and to print it out and put it on my wall. When we reach high rounds the game just shuts down, It's drawing power from my entire house and my lights start to flicker as the energy enters my computer. The breaker starts tripping like it does in phasmophobia, which BTW had a terrible update. Idk if yall have heard but they fumbled their game and made it a $5 game that is horrible and multi million dollar company doesn't care about their fanbase. Im assuming this update was worked on by an intern which reminds me of the agent monkey from BTD battles BAHAHAHHA because hes useless. We will be supporting the developers of the BTD franchise and we love the community, on a side note, Im hoping this game will not steer towards the DEI direction that many multimillion dollar companies have steered towards.  One of my biggest Idols is shroud, Im hoping shroud will be able to play this game with his good friend theburntpeanut. Speaking of peanuts, monkeys are actually allergic to peanuts and will literally blow up when eating a peanut, so do not feed it peanut butter or it will go boom. When im farming my banana farms, everytime I see a banana I yell GOOP!!! GOOP!!! GOOP!!!! because its so funny and I love theburntpeanut.  The dart monkey could've taken down Epsteins operations solo. Reminds me of the cayo perico heist from GTA 5, I always solo that heist and make so much money like my banana farms do.  Im really hoping they can bring this game to the switch 2 because I want to be playing this game non stop, even at school. I'm predicting another lockdown due to the hentai virus going around, not sure how that appeared but im sure someone was gooning to Nami from one piece, which I honestly dont blame them cause holy molies. Oda is one lucky guy. BTD 6 has such a unique playstyle that allows you to play competitively and even added a ranked mode which is MEGA sweaty LOLLLL.  The graphics remind me of Ark survival ascended. I was wondering if there is going to be a BTD 7 where we play as the dart monkey and our master upgrades us, and we have to work to please our master as he keeps upgrading me and making me more powerful. BTD6 developers, I really hope you read my review because I am your number 1 fan, actually a MEGA FAN LOLLL,  I know every hotkey for every tower. Thank you and goodbye and have a good summer with plenty of ideas for the summer so that this game can keep living and be prosperous so that your pockets can fill so that you can feed your families so that yall can put it back into the game so that we may enjoy the game so that there are more purchases on the game so that yall can keep getting more money so that yall can hire a better team so that yall can work on BTD 7 so that yall can max yalls revenue stream. Thank you

I expect you to read that in one breath. I HATE SOCIAL SESON

As I pass 100 hours I felt obligated to write a review. I am surprised and impressed with how much I got out of this game. I've always loved TD games but never tried the Bloons series until I got this on sale thinking it would be a fun little time waster for a few hours. I didn't anticipate the staggering amount of content for someone willing to 100% the game. The achievements are all challenging but doable, the challenges have forced me to reconsider strategies and try new towers and builds rather than use the same method repeatedly, and the various game modes have provided at least 100 hours of entertainment so far with many more on the horizon. I havent even touched the co-op aspect which I hope to do if I get some friends into the game. Frankly I was expecting a silly flash game, but ended up with one of the best TDs I've played. Well worth the money

great game with such predatory monetization; most recent update brings in a new hero that is locked for 2 weeks behind a bundle that has he-man collab items to artificially increase the price, this is on top of every other thing that's P2W

One of the best bang-for-your-buck games full stop. The definitive tower defense game. High recommend.

The Social Season event is ♥♥♥♥ awful and why I'm thumbs downing this review. Two weeks is NOT enough time for it, I feel like a month is not even good enough, and I put in over 40 hours specifically dedicated to it. Its exhausting, and its the first time in 600 hours of gameplay that I went from adoring this game to thinking it ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sucks and that I regret those 40 hours. Entirely. There is no reward that makes up for that.
the start of the game impoppable mode is really hard do i would recommend playing easy mode and leveling up the chararters you have not leveled up
Você começa o jogo, põe macacos no mapa, tudo parece simples. Você pisca os olhos e você agora está estudando sobre economia de bananas e como você pode derrotar o moab da rodada 40 com 300 de grana.

It is absolutely atrocious to see where this game is now. As someone who got this game during the Steam 2021 Winter Sale, I have watched this game become loaded with micro transactions. Now there is a He man hero event where you are forced to cough up $16.99 in real money to get access to a limited time hero and skin. 2 entire game modes are ~$12.99 each. I can't recommend doing more than the base maps, odyssey (if it doesn't bore you), bosses with friends, and co op. Co op is the main selling point of this game for me, every other mode added is really just mobile game filler.
The game play is amazing, probably the best tower defence there is, but locking a hero behind a paywall is gross and disappointing

best tower defense I ever played! The Updates are really good and it's very replayable as they keep adding new towers to unlock, there's always a challenge with the 156 achievements and the I think 20-30 maps you can black border but the hero's are very expensive because the currency is not that common
Falsely accused of hacking and was mocked by support when asking for an appeal or specific software it flagged on my pc.

Unlike most other mobile games, the devs strive for actual balance. Every tower in the game sees a use case at least once in the base maps, while still enabling the user's favorites to be applicable. The boss events are a load of fun, the skins and monkey knowledge give a reason to keep playing, and there's many ways to customize your profile. The only things that I'm not a huge fan of is the lack of interesting gameplay in the odyssey events and how they've basically forgotten about map maker... the other, more interesting parts of the game make up for that though.

Bloons TD 6 is fun at first and the gameplay is still really solid, but after a while it starts feeling way too grindy. Unlocking everything takes forever, and a lot of the progression turns into repeating the same maps and strategies over and over again.

The tower variety and updates are great, but the grind can make the game feel more like a chore than a strategy game sometimes. It’s still enjoyable with friends or for casual matches, just not something I can nonstop grind anymore.

At some point you stop popping bloons for fun and start popping them because you’ve already committed too many hours.
This slop keeps updating with more pay to get slop microtransactions, breaking all the mods every time with uppdates that I for sure as hell am NOT going to pay for.

Social season is just a grind for the sake of grinding. There's no point in beating the same stage 50 times using only supports or having you complete 30 times stages that take around half an hour each in a two week event, other than just artificially expanding the playtime.

Tl;dr This company no longer respects your time or money. They added achievements that require having no life outside of the game.

If you played any of the BTD series on flash websites back in the day, then this game is a good build off of those originals. More monkey types, Heroes, a good variety of maps, etc.

HOWEVER

This newest update that introduced "Social Seasons" is a huge slap in the face of players. It is full of lies and manipulation tactics, all to absolutely try to get you to spend real money to get this done in any reasonable time. Let's go through the announcement line by line here.

"Each Social Season is a new Team-focused event where participating Team members complete individual goals to earn points and progress toward special new Team rewards."
-So far so good. And the team rewards being temporary perks are neat. But, it only lasts until the next social season.

"Social Seasons have 2 Phases over two weeks each, with 5 stages of rewards."
-First lie. You get two weeks for the entire thing, not each phase.

"Goals are balanced to require some daily effort from most of a large team, with max rewards still being possible for a small dedicated Team." 
-Oh man this one is a doozy. Max rewards are at 175,000 points. An individual can get 15,000. You earn points in 5 steps on each phase. You earn 40, 80, 180, 400, then 800. 1500 points per objective, 5 objectives per phase. A team maxes out at 15 members. Doing the math equals needing 13/15 members doing everything to get everything. Anything less and you CANNOT get all the rewards.
-Also, the objectives themselves are not merely "some daily effort". People have reported needing 50+ hours to do everything. Over two weeks. And some objectives require 50 games to complete fully, or specifically farm for. (ex 50 maps completed with only support monkeys / activate 7500 abilities {seriously} / purchase 10 support paragons) And you cannot use freeplay to earn any of these challenges. So good luck buying paragons before round 100!

"Don’t worry, slower teams and late joiners can still work on any incomplete Phase 1 tasks during Phase 2."
-Many of the objectives cannot be completed simultaneously (ex. Only use dart monkeys / complete maps only using support monkeys.) and lot of the objectives are very time consuming. Cramming it all into one week is giving a second job to the player.


But never fear, you can purchase the fast-track feature to skip to 25% completion of maps!! Just pay $9 to make the grind slightly less tedious!! Money too tight on these objectives? Just pay $18 for double cash mode!!

Get a game that wants you to play it, not one like this that manipulates you into playing it. 50+ hours over two weeks for completing some grindy objectives is a great way to burn your playerbase out.

I got this game a few years ago on my iPad. It was kind of addicting, actually. But then, I stopped. I would sometimes play it for fun, and then just stop. Then, my brother got into this game again. Starting on his iPad and unlocking every tower and all of their upgrades(I don't know if he has all the paragons) on his iPhone. Then, he encouraged me to play it again. And guess what? It just... started crashing. On my 5 year old iPad. Then, my brother started to FORCE me to play the game again. I had to refuse. But then, he talked about the Steam version. I thought it WASN'T on Steam. I thought it was only on this weird thing called Bluestacks(or whatever). After some trials, I got the Steam version. I linked my account on my iPad to my computer, and, boom. Everything was back. And, to my notice, the game would very rarely crash. This game hasn't even crashed once in any of my games! Not even anywhere else! So, yeah. You should probably get and play this game if you like TD games that have upgrades to towers. That's all from me!

I bought this game to have something casual to play during Zoom calls. Now I have a spreadsheet dedicated to optimal pop-up radius geometry for a digital gorilla carrying a rocket launcher.Do not be fooled by the cute cartoon aesthetic. This is not a wholesome game about monkeys and balloons. This is a cold, calculated simulator of domestic warfare where you will unironically find yourself screaming at a digital screen because a camo-painted blimp snuck past your wizard. THIS GAME IS WONDERFUL

I’ve played this game on and off for the past 8 years that it’s been out. Although I would like for them to come out with another BTD, I very much enjoy BTD6. The vast types of monkeys, towers, maps, and bloons makes this game practically impossible to get bored of or not experience something new. Not only that, but they still consistently update this game on the regular, with new monkeys or heroes every update. It’s not just the traditional tower defense mode that I enjoy, there are so many other game modes that spice up the experience and allow you to explore more ways to enjoy such a nostalgic franchise.
The only thing that I kinda don’t like about this installment is the art direction; it’s not bad by any means, I just prefer the old style, even BTD5. The whole 3D aspect rips away that character and charm that the franchise had years back, but I think that’s just nostalgia coating my lens. It’s still a great looking game. Over the years, the game has gotten extremely expansive, and it's honestly overwhelming with the amount of content that it has, I kind of wish it was just simply a bloons td game with some form of progression and nothing more. Although, I do see how it appeals to some of the newer TD fans (with the Odyssey and team guild stuff).
I don’t really have much else to say about this, it’s a good TD game with lots of content. Not my favorite BTD, but it’s still good. I’d recommend it for the price (just don’t get microtransactions).
—-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pros:
Lots of monkeys
Many heroes with unique abilities
Tons of maps
Fun progression system
Voice lines ig

Cons:
Overwhelming amount of content
Too many updates
